data.title: UK's Legal & General Group Boosts Shareholder Returns After 'Strong' 2024 Results
data.description:
Legal & General Group on Wednesday unveiled a 500 million pound-sterling share buyback for 2025 amid "significant" progress with its growth strategy and "strong" 2024 financial performance.

For the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, the British financial services group's core operating profit increased to 1.62 billion pounds from the year-ago 1.53 billion pounds, with total operating profit rising 3% to 1.71 billion pounds

Profit after tax attributable to equity holders, however, fell to 191 million pounds from 457 million pounds, mainly due to a higher effective tax rate and the first-time implementation in 2024 of the UK top-tax rules. Meanwhile, the group's Solvency II coverage ratio stood at 232%, compared with 224% a year earlier.

"2024 has been a year of significant strategic progress and strong financial performance. We delivered 6% growth in our core operating profit and core EPS, alongside excellent new business volumes, while investing for the future," Chief Executive Officer Antonio Simoes said in the earnings release. "We are seeing positive commercial momentum as we execute our strategy with rigour and pace."

To streamline its business and generate sustainable growth, Legal & General introduced a refreshed strategy and new financial targets in 2024 as part of its revised business model focusing on three core businesses: Institutional Retirement, Asset Management and UK Retail.

The board declared a final dividend of 0.1536 pound per share, bringing the full-year 2024 dividend to 0.2136 pound per share, up from 0.2034 pound in 2023. "We stated at our Capital Markets Event that we intended to return more to shareholders and that is exactly what we are doing. Our clear capital allocation framework supports our plan to return over (GBP)5 billion over the next three years, through dividends and buybacks," Simoes noted.

Looking ahead to 2025, Legal & General expects continued commercial momentum at its three segments, with plans for the disposal of each of the remaining assets in its Corporate Investments portfolio.

The group's shares were trading nearly 2% lower in London as of late Wednesday morning.

data.title: Goldman Sachs flinches and lowers its S&P 500 target. The mechanics are the interesting part.
data.description:
By Steve Goldstein

Wall Street's new math: Tariffs lower stock-market valuations

It's been tough for Wall Street strategists to hold their nerve in the face of the ever-changing tariff policy.

JPMorgan reiterated its year-end S&P 500 target but said it might take a bit longer to reach. Citigroup kept its target but said U.S. stocks are no longer a buy. The Goldman Sachs team just said "enough" and cut its forecast. It's not a very big reduction. The Goldman team led by David Kostin moved their year-end S&P 500 SPX view to 6,200 from 6,500.

The mechanics are more interesting. They have moved their S&P 500 earnings-per-share estimate for 2025 slightly lower, to $262 from $268, compared with the $270 Wall Street consensus. That's in reaction to Goldman's economists earlier this week lowering their GDP view on expectations of a 10-percentage-point tariff rate increase. The simple math is that every 5-percentage-point increase in the tariff rate reduces S&P 500 earnings by 1% to 2%. The new earnings forecast also took into account elevated uncertainty and tightening financial conditions.

And that's just the EPS side. The new Goldman S&P 500 estimate also includes a lower multiple. "The headwinds to equity valuations from a spike in uncertainty are typically relatively short lived. However, an outlook for slower growth suggests lower valuations on a more sustained basis," they said.

Again, the changes were not dramatic - they lowered the multiple to 20.6 times projected earnings, versus 21.5 times previously.

Still, baked into Goldman's new numbers was an assumption that things will improve, as 6,200 is higher than Tuesday's close of 5,572. But how?

"We believe investors will require either a catalyst that improves the economic growth outlook or clear asymmetry to the upside before they try to 'catch the falling knife' and reverse the recent market momentum," they said. It is a chart crime (all dual Y-charts are), but they posted a chart of the indexed return of cyclicals vs. defensive stocks and compared it with the consensus GDP outlook, to make the case the stock market is currently pricing in a growth rate below even Goldman's below-the-Street view.

Granted, these investors might be right - or not pessimistic enough. "The key market risk going forward is a major further deterioration in the economic outlook," they said.

The median S&P 500 performance from peak-to-trough around recessions is a 24% decline, versus the 9% peak-to-trough decline the S&P 500 has experienced this year. But history offers hope: since 1980, buying the S&P 500 after a 10% drop - outside a recession - has delivered gains in six months 86% of the time

The markets

U.S. stock futures (ES00) (NQ00) were higher ahead of the CPI data.

   Key asset performance                                                Last       5d      1m       YTD     1y 
   S&P 500                                                              5572.07    -3.57%  -8.18%   -5.26%  7.67% 
   Nasdaq Composite                                                     17,436.10  -4.64%  -11.24%  -9.71%  7.20% 
   10-year Treasury                                                     4.281      0.00    -34.80   -29.50  8.70 
   Gold                                                                 2920.3     -0.30%  -0.30%   10.65%  34.00% 
   Oil                                                                  66.94      0.83%   -6.00%   -6.86%  -15.90% 
   Data: MarketWatch. Treasury yields change expressed in basis points

The buzz

Economists are expecting a 0.3% change in both the headline reading of the consumer-price index, as well as the core, which excludes food and energy.

The European Union responded to U.S. tariffs of 25% on steel and aluminum imports with countermeasures they said matches the value amount, which are set to go into effect in April.

Intel's stock (INTC) was surging on a report from Reuters that a consortium led by Taiwan Semiconductor is looking at a venture to operate the U.S. chipmaker's factories.

Roche (RHHBY) agreed a $5.3 billion licensing and development deal with Zealand Pharma in the burgeoning weight-loss category.

Best of the web

White House economic messaging spooking Trump's own advisers.

The anywhere-but-America trade has been working. But there are limits.

Nasdaq halts high-speed trading service after regulatory pressure.

The chart

RBC Capital Markets rates strategists, led by Blake Gwinn pointed out that, in recent years, soft survey data has not done its job in predicting what the hard data will say. "Part of that disconnect was that upper income consumers (who are of outsized importance to aggregate consumption) were still in good shape, with the soft data drag coming from lower-income cohorts. But if the new downturn in soft data is stemming from the upper-income cohorts, then soft data weakness could actually lead to more of a drag on the hard data," they said. "Very hypothetical at this point, but we do think the gap long seen between soft/hard data may continue to tighten."

Random reads

Also in bad airline customer news: A man going through security was caught with a live turtle in his pants.

-Steve Goldstein

data.title: Clean Power Hydrogen's Membrane-Free Electrolyzer Passes Level 2 Site Acceptance Test
data.description:
Clean Power Hydrogen completed the second level of the site acceptance test for its MFE110 electrolyzer at Northern Ireland Water's Belfast site.

The British green hydrogen technology company said Wednesday commissioning of the membrane-free electrolyzer is underway, with the final level 3 test at the water and sewerage services company's facility expected in the second quarter.

data.title: Forward prices gain on dry weather view, dip in water levels
data.description:
Nordic forward prices inched up on Wednesday, driven by forecasts of drier weather conditions and a dip in water reserves in the hydro-dependent region.

* The Nordic front-quarter baseload power contract (ENOFBLQc1) rose 0.2 euros to 21.07 euros per megawatt hour (MWh) as of 1040 GMT.

* Nordic front-year (ENOFBLYc1) firmed 0.33% at 36.17 euros per megawatt hour (MWh).

* The weather is calmer and not as wet as before along with intense wind, which may help get rid of the surplus in the hydrosystem, Axel Weyler, an originator at Entelios Trading AB said.

* Nordic water reserves available 15 days ahead (PCAEC00) were seen at 15.17 terawatt hours (TWh) above normal, compared with 17.02 TWh on Tuesday.

* Dutch and British wholesale gas prices were mixed, with colder weather spurring demand, and as the market awaits further clarity over a potential ceasefire in the Ukraine war after a recent step-up of attacks.

* "The next days will be slightly colder than normal with low precipitation activity," said LSEG meteorologist Georg Muller in a note.

* In the European carbon market, the benchmark contract (CFI2Zc1) fell 0.51 euros to 67.73 euros a tonne.

* German year-ahead power (TRDEBYc1) remained unchanged at 82.95 euros per MWh.

* The Nordic power price for next-day physical delivery (FXSYSAL=NPX), or system price, was up 12.63 euros to 100.65 euros per MWh.

data.title: Social Buzz: Wallstreetbets Stocks Mostly Higher Pre-Bell Wednesday; Tesla, Reddit to Advance
data.description:
The most-talked-about stocks in the Reddit subforum Wallstreetbets were mostly higher hours before Wednesday's opening bell.

Tesla advanced by 2.8% premarket, after closing Tuesday with a 3.8% increase.

Reddit rose by 1.8% in pre-bell hours, following a 14.4% rise from the previous session.

Nvidia was up 1.4% in premarket activity, following a 1.7% increase from Tuesday's session.

Advanced Micro Devices was 1.1% higher pre-bell, after a 0.1% increase at Tuesday's close.

Antero Midstream was up 0.8% premarket, following a 0.8% increase from the previous session.

DTE Energy advanced by 0.3% hours before market open, following a slight increase from Tuesday's session.

MicroStrategy was down 0.6% pre-bell, swinging from an 8.9% increase from the previous session.

Apple declined by 0.4% premarket, after closing Tuesday with a 2.9% fall.

data.title: Four killed in Russian strike on grain vessel in Odesa port, Ukraine says
data.description:
A Russian missile attack on Tuesday damaged a grain vessel in the Black Sea port of Odesa, killing four people, Ukrainian authorities said.

A ballistic missile struck the MJ Pinar bulk carrier that was loading wheat for Algeria, killing four Syrian nationals and injuring one other Syrian and a Ukrainian, Ukraine's Deputy Prime Minister Oleksiy Kuleba said on Telegram.

"Russia is attacking Ukraine's infrastructure, including ports, which are involved in ensuring the world's food security," Kuleba said.

Global grain merchant Louis Dreyfus Company said in an emailed statement that the vessel had been loading at its Brooklyn-Kiev terminal at Odesa port, with terminal infrastructure also damaged.

LDC said its terminal employees were safe, with the dead among the crew of the chartered vessel.

Kuleba said another vessel was also damaged, without giving further details.

Ukraine, like Russia, is a major grain exporter. It has managed to re-establish large-scale maritime exports during the war, despite Russian strikes on ports.

Chicago wheat futures , a global price benchmark, were little changed on Wednesday.

Ukraine also reported other Russian strikes overnight as fighting continues in the three-year-old war in parallel to U.S. efforts to negotiate a ceasefire.

Castellum, Inc. Hires Tanya Bassett as Vice President of Business Development and Capture Management; Reinforces Strategic Commitment to Organic Growth
data.description:
VIENNA, Va., March 12, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Castellum, Inc. (NYSE-American: CTM) ("Castellum" or "CTM"), a cybersecurity, electronic warfare, and software engineering services company focused on the federal government, announces the hiring of Tanya Bassett as Vice President of Business Development and Capture Management.

Ms. Bassett is a performance-driven business executive with over 28 years of industry experience and a proven track record of driving growth and delivering sustainable results. She is an experienced leader with demonstrated success in winning new contracts, achieving on-contract growth, and successfully interfacing with government stakeholders and industry partners. Tanya has extensive knowledge and experience in planning and supporting Department of Defense (DoD) programs to support mission-critical requirements. She will work alongside CTM’s Vice President of Business Development and Proposal Operations, Brooke Citrano.

"I'm excited to be joining the Castellum, Inc. team and look forward to building upon recent 'Business Development' (BD) successes with more and larger wins over the coming quarters," said Tanya Bassett.

"I could not be more excited and encouraged to have Tanya join our already strong senior leadership team to significantly strengthen our business development capabilities, laser-focused on our priority efforts in capturing new opportunities and contracts. Tanya Basset is a proven, highly regarded, world-class professional and leader who knows how to grow our business and company in a keenly competitive environment. She is incredibly bright, creative, sincere, and talented in every aspect of business growth, and her extensive experience and leadership will have an immediate impact on our CTM team. This should be another clear and strong indicator of our determined and uncompromising commitment and focus on our organic growth," said Glen Ives, President and Chief Executive Officer of Castellum.

About Castellum, Inc.:

Castellum, Inc. (NYSE-American: CTM) is a cybersecurity, electronic warfare, and software engineering services company focused on the federal government - https://castellumus.com/.
